expectations.text: Recommends Area2D hitbox/hurtbox pattern (not body-to-body collision)
expectations.evidence: 'Using body-to-body collision between two CharacterBody2D nodes causes physics pushing and unreliable signals. The hitbox/hurtbox pattern with Area2D is the standard Godot approach.'
'add a periodic check using get_overlapping_areas() on the PlayerHurtbox — area_entered only fires once on initial overlap'

expectations.text: Recommends building and testing incrementally (enemy, then attack, then wiring)
expectations.evidence: Three phases: 'Phase 1: Slime Enemy with Chase AI', 'Phase 2: Sword Attack', 'Phase 3: Combat System' with testing after each

expectations.text: Includes collision layer table with explicit bitmask values
expectations.evidence: Shows layers 1-5 with names but only layer numbers, no bitmask values. Uses conventional layer assignments without bitmask distinction.

expectations.text: Separates hurt stun duration from invincibility duration
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'invincibility timer (~1.0s)' and 'knockback: brief velocity impulse' but does not explicitly separate stun from invincibility or warn about the death spiral from blocking input.

expectations.text: Provides concrete numeric values (HP, damage, speed, durations)
expectations.evidence: 'hp: int = 3' for slime, 'hp: int = 5' for player, detection radius 120px, attack cooldown 0.4s, invincibility 1.0s

expectations.text: Mentions delegating to subagents for MCP or script writing
Asks user to playtest after implementation
expectations.evidence: Ends with 'Want me to start with Phase 1?' but never asks user to playtest or test the feel.
